findings.description: The ALL CAPS brand-name requirement (with an explicit supersession of a Feb 2026 general-guide rule) has no corresponding rule in the candidate set at all — it is entirely dropped from clustering.

payload.statement: Always write LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all written materials; this patient-marketing rule supersedes the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule.

findings.description: The parent-company sign-off/footer placement rule (Priovant Therapeutics, Inc., locked footer only, do not duplicate in body sections) is missing from the candidate rule set entirely.

payload.statement: Parent company attribution (Priovant Therapeutics, Inc.) lives only in the locked footer; do not add it within document sections.

findings.description: The trademark symbol rule as clustered does not appear to carry the governance facets stated in the source: applies only on first/most prominent mention, only in external-facing documents, and must not be repeated on subsequent mentions. Without these scope/placement facets the rule reads as unconditional, which misrepresents the source constraint.

payload.additional_constraint: do not repeat ® symbol on subsequent mentions
payload.scope: external-facing documents only; first/most prominent mention only

findings.description: The ® rule and the generic-name-format rule are tightly coupled in source (the ® rule's example explicitly relies on the generic-name parenthetical format), but no relation/dependency between the two rules is captured, risking inconsistent application if one is edited independently.
